_comment: Canonical labels for poll_results. Run scripts/canonicalise_poll_labels.py to apply. Each rule defines a scope (question_keys to include or exclude) and a list of (from_zh, from_en) → (to_zh, to_en) mappings. Both from_zh and from_en, if present, must match for the rule to fire. Edit this file when adding a new pollster, a new question_key, or when a new wave introduces a label variant that should canonicalise. The script is idempotent — running it twice on canonical data is a no-op.

rules.description: No-opinion canonicalisation across non-NCCU, non-vote-intent questions. NCCU's long series uses 無反應 / 'Non response' by historical convention and is deliberately excluded so its trend chart stays consistent with its own seeded data. Vote-intent polls are excluded because they have their own residual semantics (see the vote_intent rule below).

Vote-intent canonicalisation: strip party prefixes from candidate names, fix romanisation slips, unify residual buckets. The 'haven't picked a candidate' cluster (Don't know / No clear answer / Undecided / etc.) collapses to '尚未決定' / 'Undecided'. The 'won't vote / spoiled ballot' bucket is kept SEPARATE because it carries distinct signal (abstention vs indecision) and the My-Formosa-style polls that report both should retain the distinction.
